page: Microsoft Azure D16pls_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine belonging to the Dplsv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs powered by a 3.4 GHz Microsoft ARM Neoverse-N2 processor. The CPU configuration includes a 128 MB L3 cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 64 KB L1d cache, with no hyperthreading enabled. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, but it is premium storage capable.

In benchmark evaluations, the D16pls_v6 demonstrates strong memory performance, achieving a top-tier rating in the PassMark memory score. Uncached large-block memory bandwidth is strong, with read speeds of 310.02 GB/sec and write speeds of 124.68 GB/sec. Single-threaded gzip compression also registers in the top 25% at 33.11 GB/sec. Other computational metrics, including stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU performance, Geekbench scores, and Redis SET operations, fall within the average range. Large-model LLM inference performance is a notable bottleneck, with prompt processing for a medium 7B model rated as weak.

The low-memory profile of 2.0 GB per core positions this server as a cost-efficient option for compute-centric workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. By utilizing ARM architecture, it offers a favorable performance-per-watt and resource density tradeoff. This server is well-suited for applications such as web serving, containerized microservices, software compilation, and general-purpose ARM development.
description: Microsoft Azure D16pls_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine from the Dplsv6 family, powered by a 3.4 GHz Microsoft ARM Neoverse-N2 processor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs with no hyperthreading, paired with 32.0 GB of RAM, yielding a low-memory ratio of 2.0 GB per core. While it lacks local storage and GPUs, it is premium storage capable. In benchmarks, the server excels in memory performance, securing a top-tier PassMark memory score and strong uncached large-block read/write bandwidth. General CPU performance, web serving, and Redis operations align with average tiers, though large-model LLM inference remains a bottleneck. This configuration provides a cost-efficient, compute-focused profile ideal for web serving, software compilation, and containerized ARM workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D16pls_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated Neoverse-N2 cores, 32 GB of RAM, and top-tier memory bandwidth, optimized for compute-focused workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D16pls_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine with 16 dedicated Neoverse-N2 cores and 32 GB RAM, optimized for compute-focused workloads.
tagline: An ARM-powered virtual machine featuring sixteen dedicated cores and high-bandwidth memory optimized for compute-intensive, low-memory workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 3.4 GHz Microsoft ARM Neoverse-N2 processor with 16 dedicated cores.
Equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, offering a low-memory 2.0 GB per core ratio.
Delivers top-tier memory bandwidth and strong uncached read/write speeds.
Lacks local storage but supports premium external storage options.
Best suited for web serving, software compilation, and containerized ARM applications.
